title: Ludolfia tecta
intro: Ludolfia tecta, also known as the "hidden water lily," is a type of aquatic plant belonging to the family Nymphaeaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Ludolfia tecta include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal effects. Ludolfia tecta is often used to make herbal preparations such as tea, infusions, and extracts, which are commonly consumed in traditional Asian medicine to promote health and well-being.
taxonomy: Ludolfia tecta is classified within the family Nymphaeaceae. This family is a group of aquatic plants commonly known as water lilies. Ludolfia tecta is also known as Nymphaea micrantha and is native to tropical Asia. Further classification places Ludolfia tecta within the genus Nymphaea. The plant is an aquatic, flowering perennial with adaptations for underwater growth and reproduction.
